title: Don’t Make These Mistakes When Buying Butter

chunks.title: Introduction to Butter and Cooking Fats
chunks.summary: The video begins with an overview of the speaker's preferences for butter and cooking fats, emphasizing the importance of using raw or grass-fed butter. It highlights the health benefits of unpasteurized milk products over their pasteurized counterparts.
chunks.transcript: this is everything you need to know about butter and cooking fats what I use and how I shop for it so you can see this grocery store has a large selection of Butters and my favorite is going to be this raw butter so I prefer butter that is from milk that has not been heated pasteurization of milk the heating of milk changes the confirmational structure of the proteins probably the way protein is the protein that we care about the most it's pretty clear that kids who grow up drinking unpasteurized unheated milk have lower rates of asthma eczema and allergies there's multiple studies that show that unpasteurized unheated milk is healthier than pasteurized milk so if I can find a raw butter that is going to be my first

chunks.title: Choosing the Right Fats for Cooking
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses various cooking fats, favouring butter, tallow, and ghee while cautioning against olive and avocado oils when heated. The importance of high-quality oils is stressed, along with a brief mention of the challenges in finding pure products.
chunks.transcript: choice if I can't find a raw butter I'm going to look for something that's grass-fed and organic in this grocery store it's hard to find both but there are a couple of options you can get organic butter that says it's from pasture raised cows probably not entirely grass-fed and you can get grass-fed butter from Ireland so both of these are good options if you can't find a raw butter in terms of salted versus unsalted doesn't really matter whatever you prefer I'm usually going to end up adding salt to the foods I use butter in anyway butter is one of my favorite cooking fats when I'm heating things in a pan I don't use seed oils obviously but I don't use olive oil or avocado oil either because those oils are also going to oxidize they're also going to degrade when you heat them in a pan Olive and avocado are not great for cooking at all interestingly take note of the fact the major majority of olive oils and the majority of avocado oils are also cut with seed oil so if you're going to use olive oil and avocado oil don't heat it and make sure it's a high quality oil what do I like for cooking fats I like Tallow which is rendered beef fat I like butter and I like ghee people prefer ghee sometimes because it has a higher smoke point than butter because many of the milk proteins have been removed and if you like Tallow I've got great news for you we are going to make the best Tallow on the market from lineage Provisions check us out provisions.com we currently make the best meat sticks in the world the only meat sticks with liver and heart the only meat sticks with organs grassfed grass finish and we're going to make the best Tallow coming very soon check it out guys couple things to watch out for with butter KY gold butter recently had a scandal where they weren't really fully

chunks.title: Misleading Plant-Based Products
chunks.summary: This section focuses on plant-based butters and margarine, criticizing their health implications and the misleading labels used in marketing. The speaker emphasizes that these products often contain unhealthy seed oils and lack the nutrients found in animal-based butters.
chunks.transcript: grass-fed and there were pfas forever chemicals found in the packaging of many car gold Butters also when you look at the butter aisle I don't think any of you guys will fall for this but for some reason they put plant Butters in with the regular Butters this is essentially new age margarine and complete they're all full of seed oils and other garbage that you don't want so if you fear butter you should not fear butter butter is a health food and contains so many vital nutrients for humans steric acid odd chain fatty acids like C15 and c17 are found in butter these are healthy for humans consistently associated with good outcomes in humans there are multiple observational trials associating dairy fat with leanness butter also contains cla a compound associated with leanness and improved weight loss but plant-based Butters don't contain any of that no vitamin K2 this stuff is garbage let's see what's on this one plant-based oil blend from canola coconut and sunflower oils so two out of the three are seed oils there I don't even know how they can call it butter there's some kind of fugazy BS going on in in labeling of foods they call things eggs and plant Butters that are not actually real this stuff is full of four different seed oils look at this one Earth Balance that's about the most hippie sounding margin I've ever heard heard of four different seed oils palm oil canola oil soybean oil and flax seed oil all garbage and not things you want in your life don't be fooled by plant-based Butters or Oils I know you guys won't be but just a reminder

chunks.title: Butter Consumption and Satiety
chunks.summary: The speaker argues that butter can be consumed liberally without fear of weight gain, contending that whole foods, such as butter and meats, promote satiety. They challenge the traditional view of calorie consumption, advocating for the quality of food over quantity.
chunks.transcript: marketing is weird what about how much butter to eat I think you can eat as much butter as you want I don't think you're going to gain weight eating butter no matter how much you eat I think that butter will feed back into your satiety mechanisms easily and if you over consume butter you just won't be hungry I know a lot of people debate me on that but I challenge any of you guys or girls to get fat eating butter butter is only going to make you full it's going to nourish you it's going to create good hormonal profiles it's going to increase your sex hormones it'll probably increase your libido butter isn't going to make you fat I won't eat only butter but eat as much butter as you want this is my controversial perspective when you are eating Whole Foods when you are eating meat and whole unprocessed plant Foods or butter and Tallow and eggs and milk or cheese you're not going to gain weight on those Foods because they don't sabotage your Sati and the calories in calories out crowd will lose their mind and explode their minds they'll say that's not true their minds will explode but here's the deal you can only overeat on whole meat and plant foods and butter for so long before your satiety mechanisms go man stop eating you're not hungry anymore I think it's very very very very hard to gain weight eating unprocessed Foods the satiety mechanisms will feedback yes if you are forced with a gun to your head you could gain weight eating these things but in any other situation where you're not being being forced to overeat or you're not looking at a study where people are forced to overeat unprocessed meat eggs dairy and plant Foods you are not going to gain weight eating these foods you can eat them until you are full this is why I'm such a fan of eating high quality foods and I don't think that you are overweight I don't think you're fat because you eat too much food I think you're fat because you

chunks.title: Intrinsic Body Intelligence in Eating
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses the body's intrinsic ability to regulate hunger and weight with regard to high-quality foods, including butter, meat, and cheese. They suggest that people can eat freely from these food groups without worrying about gaining weight.
chunks.transcript: eat the wrong Foods if you improve the quality of your foods you will lose weight full stop end of story I stand by that so how much butter can you eat as much as you want how much meat can you eat as much as you want how much cheese can you eat as much as you want how many eggs can you eat as many as you want how many oranges can you eat as many as you want because I think that your body has an intrinsic intelligence and will feed back and you will not be hungry from those Foods obviously if you're diabetic if you're obese if you're metabolically unwell your body is not going to handle sugar as well as somebody who's metabolically healthy you can pair those things back but I don't think you need

chunks.title: Conclusion and Encouragement
chunks.summary: The video concludes with a reinforcement of the speaker's views on diet, emphasizing the health benefits of natural fats. They encourage viewers to embrace butter and quality meats, and invite them to stay tuned for future products.
chunks.transcript: to completely eliminate carbohydrates even if you're diabetic that's a subject in a separate video so eat your butter that's how I shop for it those are the fats I used to cook if you guys enjoy this video please like comment and subscribe check us out in lineage Provisions for the finest meat sticks with organs on the planet and stay tuned for our Tallow which is going to knock
